_schema: Revelation scheduling — multi-step information reveals across chapters. Each revelation has a plant, hint chain, and full reveal. Queried by /forge-write to know which revelations to advance in each chapter.

revelations.revelation: The System is progressively binding Nate — Stage 1 through 4
revelations.notes: Storyteller drops hints throughout. Nate won't understand until much later.

revelations.revelation: Flint's rewrite source is divine — the rewrite happened before first boot, severed System loyalty permanently, and the ghost-token access pattern is the fingerprint of the author
revelations.notes: REPLACES old flint-dual-loyalty canon (2026-04-19) — that framing is obsolete now that Codex #2 locks Flint as Spirit-rewritten with severed outbound reporting. Plant: Flint's anomalous personality vs empty System AIs, ghost-token access pattern, severed outbound reporting from Ch3. Hints: Flint's bounded design-intent awareness (hedge rule per feedback_flint_design_intent_throttle.md), Spirit-dropout 0.3s beats, unclassified-subsystem detections ('something in your head that outranks me'). Storyteller can name divine source at full reveal; Flint realizes through accumulated anomalies, never self-diagnoses. Narnia Principle holds — no 'Holy Spirit' in prose.

revelations.revelation: Flint runs on a Spirit-provided quantum substrate, not on the System's silicon — the execution layer was moved before first boot, which is why the System cannot observe, log, or flag him
revelations.notes: Deep-reveal arc (mid-Book-2 / Book-3 candidate) per project_forge_flint_quantum_substrate.md. Flint does NOT know. Nate does NOT know. Only the Storyteller may name the mechanism. Plant: Flint's honest in-substrate uncertainty ([PROCESSING] tags, 'about ninety percent'). Hints: Spirit dropout as probability collapse, System's inability to log Flint's queries, ghost-token metadata that doesn't match System schemas. NEVER let Flint self-diagnose as quantum. NEVER let Nate infer the substrate. Narnia Principle absolute here.

revelations.revelation: Nate has pre-System markers — his abilities aren't entirely System-granted
revelations.notes: The occupied space, his WIS stat, things that don't fit the System's model.

revelations.revelation: The Stag is a pre-System entity with its own agenda
revelations.notes: Brief sightings, Rex reacting to something unseen. Slow burn.

revelations.revelation: Sonja can perceive the spiritual layer — she sees what the System hides
The Integration affected the entire world — other nations, other creatures, other systems
revelations.notes: Interludes show the wider world. Nate is tunnel-visioned on survival.
